_doc: Durable qualitative intelligence per symbol. No live numbers (so it never goes stale or misleads). Fields: biz, archetype, moat, watch[], valuation, peers[], ownership. Sector fallbacks live in code.

TCS.NS.biz: India's largest IT services firm — builds and runs software/IT systems for global banks, retailers and manufacturers, paid in dollars.
TCS.NS.moat: Scale, deep client relationships and a huge trained workforce competitors can't quickly replicate.
watch: Growth is tied to US/Europe IT budgets — a recession there slows deals
AI could compress the headcount-led billing model over time
Richly valued — leaves little room for disappointment
TCS.NS.valuation: Quality you pay up for — rarely cheap; buy on market-wide dips, not for a bargain.
TCS.NS.ownership: Promoter: Tata Sons. Heavily owned by FIIs and domestic funds; consistent buyback + dividend record.
TCS.NS.special: India's most valuable IT firm — a cash machine that returns nearly all profit via dividends + buybacks.

INFY.NS.biz: No.2 Indian IT services exporter — digital, cloud and consulting work for global enterprises, billed mostly in dollars.
INFY.NS.moat: Brand, scale and long-standing Fortune-500 client roster.
watch: Cyclical to global IT spending and US/Europe macro
Frequent senior-management churn has rattled investors before
AI-led productivity is a long-term threat to the billing model
INFY.NS.valuation: Premium but usually a touch cheaper than TCS — a relative-value play within large-cap IT.
INFY.NS.ownership: Professionally managed (no dominant promoter); high FII + mutual-fund ownership; strong buyback history.
INFY.NS.special: The bellwether of Indian IT — when Infosys cuts guidance, the whole sector moves with it.

HCLTECH.NS.biz: Large IT services + products firm, strong in infrastructure management and engineering R&D services.
HCLTECH.NS.moat: Differentiated infra/engineering mix and a generous, steady dividend.
watch: Products & platforms business can be lumpy
Same global-IT-budget and AI risks as peers
HCLTECH.NS.valuation: Usually the cheapest of the big-4 IT — appeals to value + dividend seekers.
HCLTECH.NS.ownership: Promoter: Roshni Nadar family; high institutional ownership.
HCLTECH.NS.special: The dividend king of big IT — highest payout, with a differentiated infra/engineering niche.

WIPRO.NS.biz: Global IT services firm; long turnaround story under newer leadership.
WIPRO.NS.moat: Scale, though growth has lagged TCS/Infosys for years.
watch: Has chronically grown slower than peers
Leadership/strategy changes create uncertainty
WIPRO.NS.valuation: Cheaper for a reason — a bet on the turnaround actually delivering.
WIPRO.NS.ownership: Promoter: Azim Premji (among India's biggest philanthropists); large buybacks.
WIPRO.NS.special: The perennial turnaround — cheapest of the big-4 IT, a bet on a long-awaited revival.

HINDUNILVR.NS.biz: India's biggest consumer-goods company — soaps, shampoos, detergents, tea, foods used in most Indian households daily.
HINDUNILVR.NS.moat: Unmatched distribution reach (millions of outlets) and iconic brands.
watch: Rural demand and raw-material (palm oil, crude) costs swing margins
Premium valuation for low-double-digit growth
Small local/D2C brands chipping at categories
HINDUNILVR.NS.valuation: A 'sleep-well' defensive — almost never cheap; you pay for predictability.
HINDUNILVR.NS.ownership: Promoter: Unilever (global FMCG giant); widely held by funds.
HINDUNILVR.NS.special: Reaches ~9 of 10 Indian households — the deepest distribution moat in consumer goods.

NESTLEIND.NS.biz: Maker of Maggi, KitKat, Nescafé and infant nutrition — premium packaged foods.
NESTLEIND.NS.moat: Powerhouse brands (Maggi is near-synonymous with instant noodles) and pricing power.
watch: Heavy reliance on a few mega-brands (Maggi)
Food-safety/regulatory headlines can hit hard (recall history)
Expensive — priced for perfection
NESTLEIND.NS.valuation: Premium defensive; quality is not in doubt, the price usually is.
NESTLEIND.NS.ownership: Promoter: Nestlé S.A.; high dividend payout.
NESTLEIND.NS.special: Maggi alone is a near-monopoly — extraordinary pricing power in packaged food.

BRITANNIA.NS.biz: India's leading biscuits & bakery company (Good Day, Marie, bread, dairy).
BRITANNIA.NS.moat: Dominant biscuit distribution and brand recall.
watch: Wheat/milk/packaging cost inflation squeezes margins
Intense competition in low-price biscuits
BRITANNIA.NS.valuation: Quality FMCG at a premium — steady rather than explosive.
BRITANNIA.NS.ownership: Promoter: Wadia Group; strong dividend record.
BRITANNIA.NS.special: Owns the Indian biscuit aisle — Good Day and Marie are default-choice brands.

ITC.NS.biz: Cigarettes-led conglomerate that also sells foods (Aashirvaad, Sunfeast), hotels, paper and agri.
ITC.NS.moat: Cigarette cash-machine funds a growing FMCG arm; one of India's highest dividend yields.
watch: Cigarette taxation/regulation is a permanent overhang
ESG funds avoid tobacco exposure
FMCG/hotels still lower-margin than cigarettes
ITC.NS.valuation: Cheaper than pure FMCG names + fat dividend — a value/income pick.
ITC.NS.ownership: No single promoter; large government-linked (LIC/SUUTI) and FII holding.
ITC.NS.special: A cigarette cash-machine quietly funding one of India's fastest-growing FMCG arms — with a fat dividend.

ASIANPAINT.NS.biz: India's dominant decorative-paints company — the brand most people repaint their homes with.
ASIANPAINT.NS.moat: Best-in-class dealer network and supply chain; decades of category leadership.
watch: New deep-pocketed entrant (Grasim/Birla Opus) threatens the cosy market share
Crude-linked input costs swing margins
Premium valuation with slowing growth
ASIANPAINT.NS.valuation: Historically a market darling — quality at a high price; the competitive threat is the new variable.
ASIANPAINT.NS.ownership: Promoter: founding families; high FII + fund ownership.
ASIANPAINT.NS.special: ~50%+ of decorative paints — a dealer network rivals spent decades failing to crack (Birla's now trying).

BERGEPAINT.NS.moat: Strong No.2 distribution, but a clear step behind Asian Paints.
watch: Same new-entrant and crude-cost risks as the sector
Perpetual No.2 — less pricing power than the leader
BERGEPAINT.NS.valuation: Premium like the sector; smaller and slightly cheaper than Asian Paints.
BERGEPAINT.NS.special: The solid No.2 in paints — rides the same housing demand as the leader, at a lower price.

RELIANCE.NS.biz: India's largest company — oil refining & petrochemicals, plus Jio (telecom) and Reliance Retail; building new energy.
RELIANCE.NS.moat: Scale across energy, the largest telecom subscriber base (Jio) and the largest retailer.
watch: Refining/petchem earnings swing with global crude and margins
Heavy capex cycles and historically high debt phases
Value depends on eventual Jio/Retail listings being unlocked
RELIANCE.NS.valuation: Sum-of-parts story — consumer (Jio/Retail) growth vs cyclical energy base.
RELIANCE.NS.ownership: Promoter: Ambani family; the most widely held stock in India.
RELIANCE.NS.special: India's biggest company and most widely-held stock — owns the largest telecom (Jio) and retailer.

HDFCBANK.NS.biz: India's largest private-sector bank — lends to retail and businesses, runs deposits and payments.
HDFCBANK.NS.moat: Best-in-class deposit franchise and credit underwriting; huge branch + digital reach.
watch: Post-HDFC merger, deposit growth and margins are the swing factor
A large bank can only grow so fast — law of large numbers
Asset quality always worth watching in any downturn
HDFCBANK.NS.valuation: Rare phase of trading near long-term-average valuations — a watch for patient buyers.
HDFCBANK.NS.ownership: No single promoter; the most-owned bank by FIIs and domestic funds.
HDFCBANK.NS.special: India's gold-standard private bank — and at a rare, reasonable valuation after the HDFC merger.

ICICIBANK.NS.biz: Large private bank — retail + corporate lending, strong digital push.
ICICIBANK.NS.moat: Improved underwriting and tech have driven a multi-year re-rating.
watch: Corporate-loan cycle quality
Margins compress when rates fall
ICICIBANK.NS.valuation: Re-rated on execution — quality private bank, no longer cheap.
ICICIBANK.NS.ownership: No single promoter; high institutional ownership.
ICICIBANK.NS.special: The comeback story of Indian banking — cleaned up and re-rated on near-flawless execution.

KOTAKBANK.NS.biz: Conservative, high-quality private bank known for cautious lending.
KOTAKBANK.NS.moat: Pristine asset quality and a strong liabilities franchise.
watch: Founder/CEO transition (Uday Kotak stepped back) is a key uncertainty
Conservatism can mean slower growth than peers
KOTAKBANK.NS.valuation: Premium for quality; growth-vs-caution is the debate.
KOTAKBANK.NS.ownership: Promoter holding being reduced per RBI norms.
KOTAKBANK.NS.special: The most conservatively-run big bank — pristine loan book, now navigating life after its founder-CEO.

SBIN.NS.biz: India's largest bank — government-owned; vast reach across the country.
SBIN.NS.moat: Unmatched scale, deposit base and government backing.
watch: Public-sector bank — government can direct lending/priorities
Asset quality more cyclical than top private banks
SBIN.NS.valuation: Cheap vs private peers — a value play on India's credit cycle.
SBIN.NS.special: India's banking giant at a fraction of private-bank valuations — the value play on the credit cycle.

AXISBANK.NS.biz: Large private bank — retail + corporate lending.
AXISBANK.NS.moat: Rebuilt underwriting and retail franchise after past stumbles.
watch: Patchier track record than HDFC/ICICI historically
Integration of acquired businesses
AXISBANK.NS.valuation: Often the cheapest of the big-4 private banks — relative-value pick.
AXISBANK.NS.ownership: No single promoter; large LIC/institutional holding.
AXISBANK.NS.special: Often the cheapest big private bank — a re-rating candidate if execution holds up.

BAJFINANCE.NS.biz: India's premier consumer-lending NBFC — finances phones, appliances, two-wheelers and personal loans at the point of sale.
BAJFINANCE.NS.moat: Massive customer database and underwriting/analytics edge; scale in consumer credit.
watch: Highly sensitive to credit cycles — defaults rise in a slowdown
Very high valuation prices in years of fast growth
Rising regulation of unsecured/consumer lending
BAJFINANCE.NS.valuation: Expensive growth — superb franchise, but the price assumes continued rapid compounding.
BAJFINANCE.NS.ownership: Promoter: Bajaj Group; heavy FII ownership.
BAJFINANCE.NS.special: India's consumer-lending machine — finances purchases at the checkout for tens of millions.

BAJAJFINSV.NS.biz: Holding company for Bajaj Finance plus insurance (life + general) joint ventures.
BAJAJFINSV.NS.moat: Owns one of India's best lenders and growing insurance franchises.
watch: Holdco discount — trades below sum of its parts
Insurance + lending cycle exposure
BAJAJFINSV.NS.valuation: A way to own Bajaj Finance + insurance optionality, usually at a discount.
BAJAJFINSV.NS.special: Owns Bajaj Finance plus fast-growing insurance — usually available at a holdco discount.

MARUTI.NS.biz: India's largest car maker — the everyday small/mid car brand for millions.
MARUTI.NS.moat: Largest dealer + service network and brand trust in mass-market cars.
watch: Auto sales are cyclical — tied to incomes, rates, fuel prices
Late to EVs vs newer rivals
Was slower in SUVs, the fastest-growing segment
MARUTI.NS.valuation: Quality cyclical — best bought when the auto cycle is down, not peaking.
MARUTI.NS.special: Roughly every second car sold in India is a Maruti — unmatched service network and resale trust.

EICHERMOT.NS.biz: Maker of Royal Enfield motorcycles — the dominant mid-size (350cc+) bike brand.
EICHERMOT.NS.moat: Cult brand and pricing power in premium motorcycles.
watch: Discretionary purchase — sensitive to consumer sentiment
New premium-bike competition (Triumph, Harley tie-ups)
Export/commercial-vehicle JV exposure
EICHERMOT.NS.valuation: Premium brand at a premium price — buy on demand dips.
EICHERMOT.NS.special: Royal Enfield is a cult brand — near-monopoly pricing power in mid-size motorcycles.

SUNPHARMA.NS.biz: India's largest pharma company — generics + a growing global specialty (branded) drug business.
SUNPHARMA.NS.moat: Scale in generics plus a higher-margin specialty pipeline (e.g., dermatology).
watch: US FDA plant inspections can hit specific facilities/revenues
US generic pricing is competitive and deflationary
Specialty R&D bets are long-dated
SUNPHARMA.NS.valuation: Re-rated on specialty success — quality pharma, fairly to richly valued.
SUNPHARMA.NS.special: India's pharma No.1, moving up the value chain into high-margin specialty drugs.

TITAN.NS.biz: Tata's lifestyle company — dominant in branded jewellery (Tanishq) plus watches and eyewear.
TITAN.NS.moat: Trust in jewellery (purity, design) where most of the market is still unbranded — a long runway to take share.
watch: Gold-price volatility affects demand and margins
Discretionary big-ticket spend — slows in downturns
Premium valuation
TITAN.NS.valuation: A consumption compounder you pay up for; the formalisation of jewellery is the long story.
TITAN.NS.special: Tanishq is formalising India's huge unbranded jewellery market — a multi-decade runway.

LT.NS.biz: India's largest engineering & construction firm — builds infrastructure, plus a large IT-services arm (LTIMindtree/LTTS).
LT.NS.moat: Unmatched execution scale on complex projects; proxy for India's infrastructure capex.
watch: Order inflow and execution pace drive earnings — lumpy
Working-capital heavy business
Government/Middle-East order dependence
LT.NS.valuation: The cleanest large-cap bet on India's capex cycle; IT subsidiaries add value.
LT.NS.ownership: No single promoter; large LIC/institutional holding.
LT.NS.special: The cleanest proxy for India's infrastructure capex — it builds what the country is betting on.

ULTRACEMCO.NS.biz: India's largest cement producer — an Aditya Birla company.
ULTRACEMCO.NS.moat: Biggest capacity and pan-India footprint — a consolidation winner.
watch: Cement demand/prices are cyclical and regional
Energy (coal/pet-coke) costs swing margins
Capacity additions can pressure prices
ULTRACEMCO.NS.valuation: Quality cyclical — best in an up-cycle for construction/housing.
ULTRACEMCO.NS.special: India's biggest cement maker — the consolidation winner as the industry concentrates.

PIDILITIND.NS.biz: Maker of Fevicol and adhesives — near-monopoly in Indian glues/sealants.
PIDILITIND.NS.moat: Fevicol is synonymous with adhesive — extraordinary brand pricing power.
watch: Crude-derivative (VAM) input-cost swings
Premium valuation for a mid-teens grower
PIDILITIND.NS.valuation: A 'monopoly' compounder you rarely get cheap.
PIDILITIND.NS.special: Fevicol IS glue in India — a brand so dominant it's a verb, with rare pricing power.

DMART.NS.biz: Avenue Supermarts — runs DMart, India's most profitable value grocery/retail chain.
DMART.NS.moat: Relentless low-cost operations and owned-store model deliver industry-best margins.
watch: Online/quick-commerce (Blinkit, Zepto) threatens the store model
Very high valuation prices in years of store expansion
Slower store rollout than the market wants
DMART.NS.valuation: Premium retailer — superb operator, expensive stock.
DMART.NS.special: India's most profitable grocer — a ruthless low-cost, owned-store model rivals can't match.

TRENT.NS.biz: Tata retailer behind Westside and the fast-growing value-fashion chain Zudio.
TRENT.NS.moat: Zudio's fast, affordable fashion is scaling explosively.
watch: Very richly valued after a huge run
Fashion retail is competitive and trend-sensitive
TRENT.NS.valuation: Priced for rapid growth — a momentum favourite, low margin of safety.
TRENT.NS.special: Zudio is scaling fast-fashion explosively — one of the market's favourite growth stories.

BEL.NS.biz: Bharat Electronics — defence-PSU making radars, electronics and systems for India's military.
BEL.NS.moat: Preferred indigenous supplier riding India's defence-localisation (Atmanirbhar) push.
watch: Revenue depends on government defence orders and budgets
PSU — government can influence pricing/dividends
Re-rated a lot; execution must keep up
BEL.NS.valuation: Structural defence-capex story; PSU re-rating means it's no longer cheap.
BEL.NS.ownership: Promoter: Government of India; strong dividend payer.
BEL.NS.special: The preferred indigenous supplier for India's defence-electronics push — a Make-in-India winner.

HAL.NS.biz: Hindustan Aeronautics — defence PSU making and servicing military aircraft (Tejas, helicopters).
HAL.NS.moat: Monopoly position in India's military aircraft manufacturing & maintenance.
watch: Lumpy, large government order cycles
Execution/delivery delays have history
PSU policy risk
HAL.NS.valuation: A core defence-indigenisation play; re-rated strongly.
HAL.NS.special: Near-monopoly on India's military aircraft — it builds and services the air force's fleet.

COALINDIA.NS.biz: World's largest coal miner — supplies most of India's thermal-power coal. State-owned.
COALINDIA.NS.moat: Near-monopoly on domestic coal; gushing cash flows.
watch: Long-term energy-transition (away from coal) overhang
Government can mandate dividends/pricing
ESG funds avoid it
COALINDIA.NS.valuation: Very cheap with a high dividend yield — classic value/income, with a structural cloud.
COALINDIA.NS.ownership: Promoter: Government of India; among the highest dividend yields in the index.
COALINDIA.NS.special: Mines most of India's coal and gushes cash — among the highest dividend yields in the index.

NTPC.NS.biz: India's largest power generator — mostly thermal, expanding into renewables. State-owned.
NTPC.NS.moat: Regulated, largely assured returns on a vast generation base.
watch: Coal-heavy in an energy transition
Capex execution on the renewables pivot
PSU policy risk
NTPC.NS.valuation: Steady regulated earnings + dividend + green optionality — a defensive utility re-rating.
NTPC.NS.special: India's largest power producer — regulated, steady returns plus a growing renewables pivot.

DABUR.NS.biz: Ayurveda-led FMCG — Dabur honey, Chyawanprash, Real juices, oral & hair care.
DABUR.NS.moat: Trusted natural/Ayurvedic brands with deep rural reach.
watch: Rural-demand dependent
Input-cost inflation
Premium valuation for steady growth
DABUR.NS.special: The Ayurveda FMCG leader — trusted natural brands with deep rural reach.

MARICO.NS.biz: FMCG maker of Parachute (coconut oil), Saffola (edible oil/oats) and hair care.
MARICO.NS.moat: Category leadership in coconut & value-added hair oils.
watch: Copra/edible-oil price swings hit margins
Concentration in a few key brands
MARICO.NS.valuation: Steady defensive FMCG; premium but smaller than HUL.
MARICO.NS.special: Owns coconut oil (Parachute) and value-added hair oils — leadership in everyday staples.

TATACONSUM.NS.biz: Tata's FMCG arm — tea (Tata Tea), salt, coffee, water and growing packaged foods.
TATACONSUM.NS.moat: Strong staples brands + Tata distribution; consumer reinvention story.
watch: Tea/coffee commodity price swings
Integration of acquisitions
Premium valuation
TATACONSUM.NS.valuation: A Tata consumer re-rating story — quality at a premium.
TATACONSUM.NS.special: Tata's consumer reinvention — staples brands (tea, salt) plus a fast-growing foods push.
